channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Questlove Says ‘Michael’ Biopic ‘Captured the Soul’ of Jackson, But He Had a Few Timeline Quibbles
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: Welcome to our podcast, where we dive into the latest news and trends in the music world. Today, we're talking about the upcoming Michael Jackson biopic, "Michael", and what Questlove, the drummer for The Roots, has to say about it.
[Short music transition]
Host: As a diehard fan of the King of Pop, Questlove was initially hesitant to watch the film, but he ended up being pleasantly surprised. In an Instagram post, he wrote that the movie "strips away the spectacle and shows us the person" behind the legend. He praised the film for humanizing Michael Jackson, saying "for the first time, we aren't looking at the 'THE KING'—we're looking at a human being".
[Short music transition]
Host: Questlove even admitted to initially nitpicking the film's timeline errors, but eventually put his "weapons down" because of how well the film captured Jackson's soul. He said "they captured the SOUL of it all & gave him back his humanity". The film, directed by Antoine Fuqua, follows Michael Jackson's life from his days in the Jackson 5 to the Bad album era.
[Short music transition]
Host: The biopic features an impressive cast, including Jaafar Jackson, Michael's nephew, as the King of Pop himself. Questlove's endorsement is significant, and he's even planning to see the film "again and again". He believes the film shows that success is not just about hard work, but about having confidence in oneself and one's abilities.
[Outro music plays]
Host: That's all for today's episode. "Michael" hits theaters this Friday, April 24th. Will you be checking it out? Let us know in the comments. Thanks for tuning in, and we'll catch you in the next one.
